annotations.label: Trapezoid

anchor.snippet: is a quadrilateral that has at least one pair of parallel sides

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general definition of trapezoid as a geometric object; only the trapezoidal rule for integration uses the name.

annotations.label: Bases and legs of a trapezoid

anchor.snippet: The parallel sides are called the bases of the trapezoid

annotations.note: No trapezoid definition exists in Mathlib, so its bases/legs are not formalized.

annotations.label: Exclusive definition of trapezoid

anchor.snippet: Under an exclusive definition a trapezoid is a quadrilateral having exactly one pair of parallel sides

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize trapezoids under either convention.

annotations.label: Inclusive definition of trapezoid

anchor.snippet: Under an inclusive definition, a trapezoid is any quadrilateral with at least one pair of parallel sides

annotations.note: No quadrilateral or trapezoid hierarchy exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trapezoid as non-parallelogram quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: A trapezoid can also be defined as any quadrilateral that is not a parallelogram

annotations.note: Neither quadrilateral nor parallelogram (as a geometric figure) is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Proper trapezoid

anchor.snippet: some sources use the term proper trapezoid to describe trapezoids with exactly one pair of parallel sides

annotations.note: No notion of proper trapezoid in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isosceles trapezoid

anchor.snippet: An isosceles trapezoid is a trapezoid where the base angles have the same measure

annotations.note: Isosceles trapezoid is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isosceles trapezoid has equal legs and reflection symmetry

anchor.snippet: the two legs are also of equal length and it has reflection symmetry

annotations.note: Without an isosceles trapezoid definition, this property is not formalized.

annotations.label: Acute trapezoid

anchor.snippet: An acute trapezoid is a trapezoid with two adjacent acute angles on its longer base

annotations.note: No acute trapezoid in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Three-sided trapezoid

anchor.snippet: a three-sided trapezoid, meaning it is a trapezoid wherein two trapezoid's legs have equal lengths as the trapezoid's base at the top

annotations.note: Three-sided trapezoid is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isosceles trapezoid is convex hull of antiparallelogram

anchor.snippet: The isosceles trapezoid is the convex hull of an antiparallelogram

annotations.note: Neither isosceles trapezoid nor antiparallelogram is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Obtuse trapezoid

anchor.snippet: An obtuse trapezoid, on the other hand, has one acute and one obtuse angle on each base

annotations.note: Obtuse trapezoid is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Right trapezoid

anchor.snippet: A right trapezoid is a trapezoid with two adjacent right angles

annotations.note: Right trapezoid is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Garfield's proof of Pythagorean theorem

anchor.snippet: which was used by James Garfield to prove the Pythagorean theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib has the Pythagorean theorem but not Garfield's trapezoid-based proof of it.

annotations.label: Tangential trapezoid

anchor.snippet: A tangential trapezoid is a trapezoid that has an incircle

annotations.note: Mathlib has incenters (Geometry.Euclidean.Incenter) but no tangential trapezoid notion.

annotations.label: Condition of existence of a trapezoid

anchor.snippet: can constitute the consecutive sides of a non-parallelogram trapezoid with a and b parallel only when

annotations.note: Without a trapezoid definition, this existence criterion is not formalized.

annotations.label: Characterizations of trapezoids

anchor.snippet: the following properties are equivalent, and each implies that the quadrilateral is a trapezoid

annotations.note: No trapezoid characterizations exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Midpoints of opposite sides and diagonal intersection collinear

anchor.snippet: The midpoints of two opposite sides of the trapezoid and the intersection of the diagonals are collinear

annotations.note: This trapezoid collinearity result is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cosines of adjacent angles sum to zero

anchor.snippet: The cosines of two adjacent angles sum to 0, as do the cosines of the other two angles

annotations.note: Trapezoid-specific cosine characterization is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cotangents of adjacent angles sum to zero

anchor.snippet: The cotangents of two adjacent angles sum to 0, as do the cotangents of the other two adjacent angles

annotations.note: Trapezoid-specific cotangent characterization is not formalized.

annotations.label: Bimedian divides trapezoid into equal areas

anchor.snippet: One bimedian divides the quadrilateral into two quadrilaterals of equal areas

annotations.note: Bimedians and trapezoid areas are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bimedian length equals sum of other sides

anchor.snippet: Twice the length of the bimedian connecting the midpoints of two opposite sides equals the sum of the lengths of the other sides

annotations.note: Not formalized; no bimedian notion in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Further equivalent conditions for parallel sides

anchor.snippet: the following properties are equivalent, and each implies that opposite sides a and b are parallel

annotations.note: Trapezoid characterizations are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Midsegment (median) of a trapezoid

anchor.snippet: The midsegment or median of a trapezoid is the segment that joins the midpoints of the legs

annotations.note: Mathlib has no trapezoid midsegment definition.

annotations.label: Midsegment length formula

anchor.snippet: Its length m is equal to the average of the lengths of the bases a and b of the trapezoid

annotations.note: Midsegment length formula for trapezoids is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Height (altitude) of a trapezoid

anchor.snippet: The height (or altitude) is the perpendicular distance between the bases

annotations.note: No trapezoid height definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Height from four side lengths

anchor.snippet: the height of a trapezoid h can be determined by the length of its four sides using the formula

annotations.note: Formula not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area of a trapezoid

anchor.snippet: of a trapezoid is given by the product of the midsegment (the average of the two bases) and the height

annotations.note: No trapezoid area formula appears in Mathlib (only the trapezoidal rule for integrals).

annotations.label: Bhāskara I's area formula

anchor.snippet: Bhāskara I derived the following formula for the area of a trapezoid with consecutive sides

annotations.note: Bhāskara I's trapezoid area formula is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reduction to Heron's formula

anchor.snippet: this formula reduces to Heron's formula for the area of a triangle

annotations.note: Heron's formula itself is not present in Mathlib (grep finds no occurrences).

annotations.label: Heron-like area formula

anchor.snippet: Another equivalent formula for the area, which more closely resembles Heron's formula

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bimedian bisects area

anchor.snippet: The bimedian connecting the parallel sides bisects the area

annotations.note: Not formalized; bimedian notion absent.

annotations.label: Triangle to midpoint of leg bisects area

anchor.snippet: Any triangle connecting the two ends of one leg to the midpoint of the other leg is also half of the area

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diagonal length formula

anchor.snippet: The lengths of the diagonals are

annotations.note: Diagonal length formula for trapezoids not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Areas of triangles formed by diagonals

anchor.snippet: the area of

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Harmonic mean of bases through diagonal intersection

anchor.snippet: is the harmonic mean of the lengths of the bases

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Line through diagonal and extended side intersections bisects bases

anchor.snippet: The line that goes through both the intersection point of the extended nonparallel sides and the intersection point of the diagonals, bisects each base

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Center of area of a trapezoid

anchor.snippet: The center of area ( center of mass for a uniform lamina ) lies along the line segment joining the midpoints of the parallel sides

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ratio in which center of area divides segment

anchor.snippet: The center of area divides this segment in the ratio

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Angle bisector intersection property

anchor.snippet: If the angle bisectors to angles A and B intersect at P , and the angle bisectors to angles C and D intersect at Q

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trapezoidal rule

anchor.snippet: approximating the region under the graph of the function by a collection of trapezoids, so this method is called the trapezoidal rule

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `trapezoidal_integral` for numerical integration via the trapezoidal rule.

annotations.label: Keystone effect

anchor.snippet: When any rectangle is viewed in perspective from a position which is centered on one axis but not the other, it appears to be an isosceles trapezoid

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spherical or hyperbolic trapezoid

anchor.snippet: A spherical or hyperbolic trapezoid is a quadrilateral with two opposite sides, the legs, each of whose two adjacent angles sum to the same quantity

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Saccheri quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: each of the resulting two pieces is an isosceles trapezoid with two right angles, called a Saccheri quadrilateral

annotations.note: Grep finds no Saccheri quadrilateral in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lambert quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: each of the resulting four pieces is a quadrilateral with three right angles called a Lambert quadrilateral

annotations.note: Lambert quadrilateral is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trapezoidal number

anchor.snippet: A trapezoidal number is a set of positive integers obtained by summing consecutively two or more positive integers greater than one

annotations.note: No trapezoidal number definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Crossed ladders problem

anchor.snippet: A crossed ladders problem is the problem of finding the distance between the parallel sides of a right trapezoid

annotations.note: Crossed ladders problem is not formalized in Mathlib.
